title: Communication essentials
description: Useful words for speaking, presenting, and interacting with an audience.

items.meaning: to explain thoughts in an easy-to-understand way
items.example: A strong speaker can express ideas clearly without using difficult words.
A confident introduction can make a strong impression on the audience.

items.meaning: to keep people focused and interested
items.example: Good examples and stories help a presenter hold attention.

items.meaning: to answer what other people ask
items.example: Public speakers should be ready to respond to questions calmly.

items.meaning: to convince people through clear speaking
items.example: Lawyers often need to speak persuasively in front of others.

items.meaning: the ability to make your voice strong and clear
items.example: Teachers need good voice projection in a large classroom.
